M. J. Brock & Sons, lnc.
Controciors/Developers
6767 Foresl Lown Drive

Wayne Imberi
Secretary of State

1230 J Street, Room 219
Sacramento, California 95812-1 467
Certified Mail
Return Receipt
Dear Mr. Imberi:
I am confirming the telephone conference among you, Leo Gutierrez
of our office and myself on Friday, January 26,1990 to repeat our
filing 
obligation and to provide you with a reference document
should you need to contact me again.
We questioned the specific reporting obligation of a joint venture
partnership when it qualifies as a major donor committee. Our fact
situation is that Brock performs some of its business operations
through California general partnerships. These partnerships are
comprised of each partner owning fifty 
percent (50t) of the
partnership with mutual control and mutual decision making. There
is not a dominant partner due to a majority of ownership or control.
Given these facts, we wanted to know if: (a) the joint venture files
a Form 461 thereby eliminating the need for the individual partners
to report these contributions on each one's individual Form 461 (if
the criteria 
is met), or, (b) if 
each partner reports these
contributions on each one's individual Form 461 thereby eliminating
the need for t,he joint venture to report. these contribuLions on a
separate Form 461, orr (c) the joint venture and individual partners
file Form 461 's reporting the same contributions thereby creating
mulLiple reporting for the same contributions.
your answer to our question was that a joint venture would file 
a
Form 461 separately in its own name without the need for the
individual pirtners to also fite separate Form 461 's for the same
contributions if 1) there is exactly fifty percent (50t) ownership
between the partners and 2l there is mutual control and mutual
decision making without a dominant or controlling partner.
None of the partnerships Brock is a member of meet the requirements
for reportiirg political 
contributions for calendar year 1989;
howevef, I want- to understand the reporting procedure should the
partnerships be required to file for calendar year 1 990 '


wayne Imberi
.fanuary 31, 1990
Page two (2)
Please contact me immediately if I have misstated the reporting
procedure. Our thanks again for your courtesy in assisting us with
these rules.
Very truly yours,
kHXW

sections.question: Given that Brock performs business operations through California general partnerships with 50% ownership and mutual control between partners: (a) should the joint venture file a Form 461 thereby eliminating the need for individual partners to report on their individual Form 461s, or (b) should each partner report contributions on their individual Form 461s eliminating the need for joint venture reporting, or (c) should both the joint venture and individual partners file Form 461s reporting the same contributions thereby creating multiple reporting?
sections.conclusion: A joint venture would file a Form 461 separately in its own name without the need for the individual partners to also file separate Form 461s for the same contributions if: 1) there is exactly fifty percent (50%) ownership between the partners and 2) there is mutual control and mutual decision making without a dominant or controlling partner.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a 50/50 California general partnership with mutual control and decision-making should file a Form 461 as a major donor committee separate from its partners, or whether the individual partners should report the contributions instead.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The joint venture should file Form 461 separately in its own name without individual partners needing to file separate Form 461s, provided the partnership meets the criteria of 50/50 ownership with mutual control and no dominant partner.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence letter from a company to the Secretary of State confirming and documenting a prior telephone conference where advice was given. The question and conclusion were synthesized from the body of the letter rather than from labeled sections. No formal FPPC response letter structure is present, making this technically an incoming request with referenced prior advice rather than a direct FPPC advice letter response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Given that Brock performs business operations through California general partnerships with 50% ownership and mutual control between partners: (a) should the joint venture file a Form 461 thereby eliminating the need for individual partners to report on their individual Form 461s, or (b) should each partner report contributions on their individual Form 461s eliminating the need for joint venture reporting, or (c) should both the joint venture and individual partners file Form 461s reporting the same contributions thereby creating multiple reporting?

CONCLUSION: A joint venture would file a Form 461 separately in its own name without the need for the individual partners to also file separate Form 461s for the same contributions if: 1) there is exactly fifty percent (50%) ownership between the partners and 2) there is mutual control and mutual decision making without a dominant or controlling partner.

embedding.summary: M.J. Brock & Sons, Inc. seeks clarification on the Form 461 filing obligations for California general partnerships they participate in where ownership and control are equally shared. The company confirms advice from the Secretary of State's Political Reform Division that such partnerships should file Form 461s separately without requiring individual partner reporting.
